title: ICE Agent Christian Castro Arrested in Texas on Assault Charges
excerpt: ICE agent Christian Castro, charged in the January 14 Minneapolis shooting of Venezuelan man Julio Sosa-Celis, was arrested in Harlingen, Texas by Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension investigators. DHS called the arrest a 'political stunt.'
content:
Minnesota's Hennepin County Attorney's Office announced on May 29, 2026, that ICE agent Christian Castro had been taken into custody in Harlingen, Texas, on charges stemming from a January 14 shooting during Operation Metro Surge.

  • Castro faces four counts of second-degree assault and one count of falsely reporting a crime for shooting Julio Sosa-Celis in the leg through a closed front door while four adults and two children were inside the home.
  • City surveillance video showed the entire struggle lasted approximately 12 seconds — directly contradicting Castro's claim to supervisors that he was engaged in a three-minute struggle before firing.
  • Both Sosa-Celis and the second man, Alfredo Aljorna, were in the U.S. legally under Temporary Protected Status from Venezuela. Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty said the shooting was a case of mistaken identity.
  • DHS responded by calling Castro's arrest "unlawful and nothing more than a political stunt," asserting the matter should be handled federally, not by state prosecutors.

Castro is the second ICE agent criminally charged for conduct during Operation Metro Surge, following Gregory Donnell Morgan Jr., who was charged in April for felony assault involving a gun-pointing road incident.
